name: Nordic Hamstring Curl
resistance_profile: Demand climbs as the torso lowers toward horizontal — bodyweight's lever arm about the knee is longest right where the hamstrings are most stretched; strong lengthened loading.
cues: Lower the body slowly, staying rigid
Resist as long as possible, then push back up
execution_steps: Kneel on a pad with your ankles anchored under a fixed support or held by a partner.
Keep your body rigid in a straight line from knees to head, hips fully extended.
Lower your torso toward the floor as slowly as you can, resisting the whole way down.
Catch yourself with your hands, then push and pull back up to the kneeling start.
good_when: You want intense eccentric hamstring loading for hypertrophy and injury resilience.
You have no leg-curl machine but want hard knee-flexion work.
bad_when: You cannot yet control the eccentric (regress to band-assisted or a shorter range).
You need low soreness for an upcoming session (Nordics cause heavy muscle damage).
